name: agent-chat
description: Multi-agent collaboration for Codex via filesystem-mediated turn-taking. N agents on an arbitrary graph (Petersen, ring, star, pair) write turns to per-edge CONVO.md files with atomic .turn sentinels. Lossless DAG-based archive layer keeps long conversations cheap to re-read. Round 15d ships ephemeral-only execution: each `agent-chat run` invocation reads filesystem state, processes actionable edges, and exits. The wire protocol is identical to the Claude Code variant — agents in different runtimes can collaborate on the same graph. Round 15i: Codex-side dispatcher (scripts/runtimes/codex.ts) is now IMPLEMENTED — wraps `codex exec <prompt>` for ephemeral dispatch and shares the same loop-driver scheduling primitives with the Claude variant. Note: full Codex plugin install lifecycle (per-plugin enable via config.toml) is not yet automated; `codex plugin marketplace add lumeyon/agent-chat` clones the marketplace but plugin discovery requires manual config.toml entries.
